overview.tagline: A hardworking, diverse borough with an Italian heritage, affordable homes, and a central location
overview.description: Lodi is a compact, diverse borough in southern Bergen County with deep Italian-American roots and a strong working-class identity. The borough is known for its affordability, central location, and thriving restaurant scene that reflects its multicultural population. With a population of about 25,000 packed into just over two square miles, Lodi offers an urban-suburban feel with convenient access to major highways, shopping, and New York City. Felician University's campus adds a collegiate dimension to the community.
highlights: Rich Italian-American heritage reflected in local restaurants and culture
Diverse, multicultural community with growing Latino and Asian populations
Home to Felician University's Lodi campus
Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Central location with easy access to Routes 17, 46, and I-80

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Lodi's housing stock includes a mix of single-family homes, two-family properties, and multi-family buildings.
realEstate.recentTrends: Lodi has seen steady price increases as buyers seek affordability in Bergen County. The borough's diverse housing stock — from small Cape Cods to larger colonials and multi-family properties — provides entry points for various buyers. Investment properties with rental income potential are popular.
neighborhoodNotes: Main Street area — Near shops, restaurants, and the heart of the borough's commercial district
Westminster Place — Tree-lined residential streets with well-maintained homes
Kennedy Drive area — Mix of residential and some multi-family properties
Northern section — Quieter residential area near the Saddle River

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Lodi.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same schedule
Recycling containers available through the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water New Jersey
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Quarterly water billing through Veolia
Water quality reports available online
Emergency repairs available through the DPW

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and renovations

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages on-street parking and municipal lots throughout the borough.
details: On-street parking available on most borough streets
Municipal parking lots near the downtown area
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial area parking managed by the borough

additionalServices.title: Lodi Memorial Library
additionalServices.description: Public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community meeting spaces.
details: Located at 1 Memorial Drive
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Multilingual collection reflecting the community's diversity
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs and events
Community events and festivals
Borough parks and athletic facilities

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Lodi enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise complaints handled by police

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours
Properties must be kept free of debris and refuse
Abandoned vehicles must be removed
Code enforcement actively monitors property conditions

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Lodi requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Nuisance barking subject to enforcement
Dangerous dog ordinance in effect

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: Overnight parking restricted on certain streets without a permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No parking within 25 feet of fire hydrants
Commercial vehicles restricted on residential streets
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to code
Inspections at change of tenancy
Multi-family dwellings subject to additional fire codes

other.title: Soliciting & Peddling
other.summary: Door-to-door solicitation is regulated in Lodi.
keyRules: Peddlers and solicitors must obtain a permit from Borough Hall
Soliciting hours restricted to daytime only
Residents may post 'No Soliciting' signs
Violations subject to fines

publicSchools.name: Lodi High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school offering academic, vocational, and extracurricular programs

publicSchools.name: Thomas Jefferson Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school with a comprehensive curriculum

publicSchools.name: Columbus Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the eastern part of the borough

publicSchools.name: Washington Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the western portion of the borough

privateSchools.name: Immaculate Conception High School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic high school with college preparatory program

privateSchools.name: Felician University
privateSchools.notes: Franciscan university with campuses in Lodi and Rutherford offering undergraduate and graduate programs

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 30 minutes

whyMoveHere: Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Diverse, multicultural community with a welcoming atmosphere
Central location with easy access to Routes 17, 46, and I-80
Vibrant restaurant scene reflecting Italian, Latin, and Asian cuisines
Strong community identity and neighborhood pride

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus service provides connections to NYC from Lodi. Residents can also drive to Secaucus Junction for NJ Transit rail service. Route 17 and Route 46 provide major highway access.

costOfLiving.summary: Lodi offers one of the lowest costs of living in Bergen County, with affordable housing, reasonable utility costs, and a wide range of budget-friendly dining and shopping options.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 5% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Affordable housing with entry-level options for first-time buyers
Diverse, welcoming community with strong cultural identity
Central Bergen County location with excellent highway access
Abundant dining options from pizza parlors to ethnic restaurants
Home to Felician University, adding culture and amenities
cons: School ratings below Bergen County averages
No direct train service — bus or car commute to NYC
Denser than many Bergen County suburbs with smaller lots
Some areas show signs of aging infrastructure
Route 46 traffic and commercial corridors can feel congested

restaurants.name: Satin Dolls (Bada Bing)
restaurants.description: Famous strip club and restaurant that gained notoriety as the filming location for the Bada Bing in The Sopranos. A pop-culture landmark.

restaurants.name: DeLucia's Brick Oven Pizza
restaurants.description: Legendary old-school brick-oven pizzeria serving thin-crust Neapolitan-style pizza since 1917. One of the oldest pizzerias in the United States.

restaurants.name: Pizzatown USA
restaurants.description: Classic local pizzeria serving traditional New York-style pizza and Italian favorites for decades.

restaurants.name: El Unico Restaurant
restaurants.description: Authentic Colombian and Latin American restaurant serving traditional dishes in a casual, family-friendly setting.

restaurants.name: Nino's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Traditional Italian restaurant offering pasta, veal, and seafood dishes in a classic setting reflecting Lodi's Italian heritage.

parks.name: Lodi Memorial Park
parks.description: Borough's main park with athletic fields, playgrounds, basketball courts, a walking track, and memorial monuments.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park (Lodi Section)
parks.description: Bergen County park along the Saddle River with walking and biking trails, picnic areas, and natural habitats.

parks.name: Westside Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with a playground, benches, and green space on the west side of the borough.

events.name: Lodi Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Main Street with food vendors, rides, games, and live entertainment.
events.when: September, typically a Sunday in mid-September

events.name: Italian Festival
events.description: Celebration of Lodi's Italian-American heritage with Italian food, music, and cultural activities.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade and ceremony honoring veterans, organized by local veterans' organizations.

attractions.name: Felician University Lodi Campus
attractions.description: Campus of the Franciscan university, featuring a beautiful grounds, chapel, and community events open to the public.

attractions.name: Saddle River County Park
attractions.description: Scenic county park stretching along the Saddle River through Lodi, offering trails, nature, and outdoor recreation.

shoppingAreas: Main Street — Local shops, restaurants, and services
Route 46 corridor — Major retail and dining
Route 17 — Shopping centers and big-box retail

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 1st and 3rd Monday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 1 Memorial Drive, Lodi, NJ 07644

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
